dashboard.title: Dashboard
dashboard.subtitle: Overview of your pipelines and work items.

boards.title: Boards
boards.subtitle: Kanban view of your work items. Drag cards between columns to change state.
boards.columnsEmpty: Load the board first to configure columns.
boards.columnsFilterNote: Columns reflect your active filter — reload after changing filters to discover more states.
boards.resetConfirmMsg: This will clear your column config and reload the board.

actions.title: Pipelines
actions.subtitle: Browse workflows, manage runs, and monitor health.
actions.noRunsFound: No runs found for this repository.

builder.title: Chain Builder
builder.subtitle: Create and run sequences of CI/CD pipelines.
builder.noSteps: No steps yet — add one below.
builder.useLatestTagHint: Will use the latest tag from this repository at run time.
builder.gitlabPipelineNote: GitLab CI — the default pipeline (.gitlab-ci.yml) will be triggered on the selected branch.
builder.emptyState: Select a chain from the left, or create a new one.
builder.noRunsYet: No runs yet — hit Run to start.

notif.chainFail: One or more steps failed — check the logs.

settings.title: Settings
settings.subtitle: Manage your API tokens. All data is stored locally in your browser.
settings.gitlabUrlHint: Change only if using a self-hosted GitLab instance.
settings.jiraTokenHint2: Generate at id.atlassian.com → Security → API tokens.
settings.jiraProjectNotConfigured: Not configured — the sprint widget won't work.
settings.sprintNotConfigured: Not configured — Boards and the sprint widget won't work.
settings.storageSessionActive: Session only — tokens are cleared when you close the browser (8 h max).
settings.storagePersistActive: ⚠ Persistent — tokens are saved in localStorage and survive browser restarts.
settings.storageRiskMsg: ⚠ Security risk: tokens will be saved in localStorage and persist after closing the browser. Anyone with access to this device can read them. Do you accept this risk?
settings.activeCiProviderHint: Select which provider is used across Pipelines, Chain Builder and Releases.
settings.chainExecSubtitle: Controls how often the tool polls GitHub for step status.
settings.workspaceSubtitle: Export your chains, graphs, releases, and settings. Tokens are never exported.
settings.webhookSubtitle: Forward every audit entry to an HTTP endpoint (SIEM, Slack, n8n, Zapier…). The endpoint must accept CORS POST requests.
settings.webhookTestErr: ✗ Could not reach the endpoint — check the URL and CORS settings.
settings.notificationsSubtitle: Show desktop notifications when chain steps complete.
settings.notifBlocked: Notifications are blocked by the browser. Allow them in your browser settings.
settings.dangerWarning: This will remove all tokens from your browser and redirect you to Onboarding.
settings.toastDurationHint: How long toast notifications stay visible

orch.title: Chain Orchestrator
orch.subtitle: Build pipelines of chains as a visual graph — connect nodes by dragging edges.
orch.emptyState: Select a graph or create a new one.
orch.noRuns: No runs yet — hit Run to start.

releases.title: Releases
releases.subtitle: Track which tag or release is deployed in each environment.
releases.emptyState: No repositories yet — add one above.

health.title: Pipeline Health
health.subtitle: Success rate, average duration and trend for each workflow.
health.repoHint: Start typing to search your repositories.
health.emptyState: Select a repository to see its workflow health.
health.noRuns: No runs found for this repository.

blockers.title: Blockers Map
blockers.subtitle: Visualise which work items are blocking others and their transitive impact.
blockers.noDeps: No blocking dependencies found in this project.
blockers.noProject: Select a project and click Load.
blockers.noDevops: Azure DevOps not configured — go to Settings to add a token.
blockers.noJira: Jira not configured — go to Settings to add a token.

onboarding.title: Welcome to CDM
onboarding.subtitle: Connect at least one integration to get started.

security.sessionExpiredDetail: Your session timed out due to inactivity. All tokens have been cleared for security.
security.sessionTimeoutHint: Inactivity limit in session-only mode. Has no effect when persistent storage is enabled.

audit.title: Audit Log
audit.subtitle: History of key actions performed across all sessions.
audit.clearConfirm: Delete all audit log entries? This cannot be undone.

prs.title: Pull Requests
prs.subtitle: Browse open and closed pull requests and merge requests.
prs.selectRepo: Select a repository to see its pull requests.

integrations.title: Integrations
integrations.subtitle: Poll any HTTP endpoint and map its status into CDM.
integrations.emptyState: Select a source or create a new one.
integrations.name: Name
integrations.corsNote: The endpoint must allow CORS requests from this browser origin.
integrations.enabledHint: Allow this source to run in the Orchestrator.
integrations.statusPathHint2: Dot-path to the status field in the JSON response. If the response is {"result":"SUCCESS"}, type result. For nested fields like {"build":{"status":"OK"}}, type build.status.
integrations.statusMapHint: Map each raw value your API returns (left) to a CDM status (right). Examples — health endpoints: UP→success, DOWN→failure; CI/CD: success→success, failed→failure, running→running; OpenShift: Running→success, Failed→failure, Pending→running.
integrations.namePathHint2: Optional. Dot-path to a label shown in the node popup, e.g. fullDisplayName.
integrations.urlPathHint2: Optional. Dot-path to a link shown in the node popup, e.g. lastBuild.url.
integrations.orchModeHint: Controls how the orchestrator waits on this source when it runs as a node. One-time: fetches once and maps the result immediately. Poll: keeps fetching until success/failure or the poll limit is reached.
integrations.orchPollGlobalHint: Uses global poll interval and max attempts from Settings → Chain Execution.
integrations.checksHint: Define one or more fields to check. The overall status is the worst result across all checks.
integrations.customHeadersHint: Extra headers sent on every request. Useful for X-API-Key, X-Auth-Token, or any proprietary header.
integrations.testNoChecks: No field paths configured — go to Config → Checks to map the status.
